mnemonics: SAME CPUs: Symmetric Multiprocessing, Asymmetric Multiprocessing, Multi-core, Load Balancing
keyPoints: Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP): Each processor is self-scheduling from a common ready queue
Load Balancing: Distributing work evenly across all processors to maximize utilization
Processor Affinity: Attempting to keep a process running on the same processor to benefit from cached data
commonMistakes: Assuming all multiprocessor systems use symmetric multiprocessing (SMP)
Thinking that more processors always linearly improve performance (Amdahl's Law limitations)
Neglecting the importance of processor affinity for cache performance
